name: cislunar-dynamics
description: Restricted Earth-Moon mission model for Artemis II style cislunar flyby lessons. The domain reconstructs one nominal mission family from launch through high Earth orbit, translunar injection, lunar flyby, free-return coast, entry interface, and splashdown. Public trajectory functions come from one continuous launch-to-splashdown state integration and are cached by flyby-altitude family.

implementation.model: Single cached mission formulation. A continuous ODE integrates spacecraft position and velocity from launch through splashdown under Earth gravity, moving Moon gravity, and smooth tracking acceleration toward a NASA-timed nominal Artemis II reference family. The reference family preserves launch, Earth-orbit setup, translunar injection, lunar flyby, free return, entry interface, and splashdown timing anchors without switching the public trajectory between separate segment solvers.
implementation.integrator: Fourth-order Runge-Kutta (RK4) for the full launch-to-splashdown mission state.
implementation.stepSize: dt = 90 s for the cached mission solve. Public function lookup uses linear interpolation between cached samples.
implementation.storage: Per-flyby family trajectory arrays store day, position, and velocity samples for the full mission from day 0 through splashdown. Derived scalar functions reuse the same cached mission solve.
implementation.cache: Cache key is the rounded flyby-altitude family in statute miles, rounded to the nearest 50 mi. All public functions share the same cache for a given family.
implementation.coordinateSystem: Earth-centered 2D cislunar plane in kilometers. Public position functions return scene units where 1 unit = 10,000 km. Earth is centered at (0,0). The Moon position is time-dependent and uses a mean orbital period of 27.321661 days.

missionAnchors.launchToOrbit: Launch, initial insertion orbit, safe orbit, high Earth orbit, Orion separation, proximity ops, and departure setup are timed to the NASA Artemis II press kit.
missionAnchors.tli: Translunar injection occurs at mission day 1.067.
missionAnchors.flyby: Lunar SOI entry, closest approach, and SOI exit are aligned to the published Artemis II nominal timeline. Flyby altitude family is controlled by the flyAlt slider or explicit function argument.
missionAnchors.return: Return correction windows, service module separation, entry interface, and splashdown are modeled on the NASA nominal mission timeline.

note: This domain supports both explicit function arguments and slider injection. If flyAlt is omitted when calling a public function, the domain reads the current scene slider with id `flyAlt` via `_init({ getSlider })`.

flyAlt.description: Target lunar flyby altitude family in statute miles. Rounded to the nearest 50 mi for cache reuse and nominal family selection.

missionX.description: Orion x-coordinate in scene units at mission day `day`.
missionX.returns: number — x-position in scene units where 1 unit = 10,000 km.

params.name: day
params.description: Mission day from launch. Clamped to [0, 9.1].

params.name: flyAlt
params.description: Optional flyby altitude family in statute miles. Defaults to slider `flyAlt` if omitted.

missionY.description: Orion y-coordinate in scene units at mission day `day`.
missionY.returns: number — y-position in scene units where 1 unit = 10,000 km.

params.name: flyAlt
params.description: Optional flyby altitude family in statute miles.

missionVx.description: Approximate x-velocity in km/s from the mission solve.

missionVy.description: Approximate y-velocity in km/s from the mission solve.

moonX.description: Moon x-coordinate in scene units at mission day `day`.

moonY.description: Moon y-coordinate in scene units at mission day `day`.

moonTheta.description: Moon orbital phase angle in radians for the active mission family.

distEarthKm.description: Distance from Orion to Earth center.

distMoonKm.description: Distance from Orion to Moon center.

missionSpeedKmS.description: Magnitude of Orion velocity from the cached mission state.

missionBlackout.description: Line-of-sight blackout test between Orion and Earth with the Moon as occluder.
missionBlackout.returns: number — 1 if the Moon blocks line of sight to Earth, else 0.

missionPhase.description: Human-readable high-level mission phase for the given mission day.

missionMilestone.description: Human-readable mission milestone bucket aligned to the NASA Artemis II nominal sequence.
